MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Defendant Last Barnd, Inc.
Off Calendar Jan-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Timothy Hall, Esq. 310-205-3981 Law Offices for Plaintiff. (by Zoom)
David Moreshead, Esq. 213-683-9272 Munger Tolles & Olson LLP for Defendant
Court reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR #4609, 415-272-1753, taamarawillat@sbcglobal.net.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED. Mr. Hall addresses labor code 201.6, labor code 2775 and/or ABC test, contests there is a triable issue as to good faith dispute. Court comments. Mr. Moreshead responds to ambiguity, 2 prongs of test, usual course of business, no dispute, online retailer, independent contractor. Mr. Hall presents a brief rebuttal; ancillary, control, employer's right to exercise control. After full argument, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

1 - Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment.

Taken Under Submission.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR #4609, 415-272-1753, taamarawillat@sbcglobal.net, Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Timothy Hall, Esq. 310-205-3981 Law Offices for Plaintiff. (by Zoom)
David Moreshead, Esq. 213-683-9272 Munger Tolles & Olson LLP for Defendant
Court reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR #4609, 415-272-1753, taamarawillat@sbcglobal.net.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED. Mr. Moreshead contests and presents arguments; contests portions of the TR re whether there is a good faith dispute under Labor code section 203, raises 2 points on denial of MSJ and triable issue of fact; regulation; standard whether reasonable, cites case authority.
Court comments. Mr. Hall responds; court correct, objectively reasonable, misclassification argument, cites case authority, jury question, good faith part. Mr. Moreshead presents a brief rebuttal; mentions 3 cases. After full arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT LAST BRAND, INC. , A DELAWARE CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

2 - Defendant's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication.

Taken Under Submission.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)
